timeline.label: Q1 2026: net sales $945.6M; diluted EPS $0.85 vs $0.95 prior year; North America segment earnings $175.4M
FY2025 10-K: net sales $3.83B; FCF $546.0M; NA margin 24.4%; 2026 sales outlook +2–5%
Board adds 5M shares to repurchase authority; continued 10b5-1 and open-market buybacks planned for 2026
Repurchased 5.9M shares for $400.8M (avg $67.44); dividends $1.38/share (+6% Q4 increase)
Early-2025 North America pricing actions to offset input costs; gross margin 38.8% vs 38.1% prior year
Capacity expansion capex in Juarez (Mexico), McBee (South Carolina), and Lebanon TN engineering facility
China restructuring: $11.3M severance to right-size Rest of World operations
Renewed $500M revolving credit facility through August 2029 with accordion to $1B
Share count reduction continues: diluted shares 151.0M (2023) → 141.9M (2025)
Water treatment profitability improvement strategy; $6.3M NA restructuring in 2024
Heat pump and high-efficiency product lines expanded for decarbonization/regulatory tailwinds
Lochinvar boiler platform integration strengthens commercial heating franchise

industry.structure: North American water heating is an oligopoly led by A.O. Smith with wholesale distribution (~800 distributors) and national retail relationships creating entry barriers. Commercial boilers add a second moat pocket via Lochinvar.
industry.share_shift: A.O. Smith maintains leading North America share in residential and commercial water heating; heat pumps and tankless competitors pressure mix but incumbents benefit from brand, channel, and replacement installed base.
industry.trend: DOE efficiency regulations, electrification (heat pumps), and input-cost volatility are reshaping product mix; post-2024 capacity investments position for volume but capex is normalizing lower in 2025–2026.
